pageSubtitle: From Bangalore nostalgia to Mumbai's most-loved dosa spot

story.heading:
Born from nostalgia,
built with love
paragraphs: Akhil Iyer is an actor-producer from Bengaluru. Every time he flew back home, his first stop was a humble darshini on Avenue Road for a benne dosa. After 11 years in Mumbai, he couldn't find anything that came close.
He tried convincing Bangalore restaurateurs to open in Mumbai. Nobody was interested. So he and Shriya Narayan — a psychologist and his partner — decided to do it themselves. Zero F&B experience.
Akhil trained under a dosa master on Avenue Road. Batter consistency, timing, the whole craft. Months of testing in their kitchen followed.
In May 2024, they opened a 275-square-foot space in Bandra. Standing tables, no chairs, self-service — a real darshini. Queues formed from day one.

quote.text: "Great dosas depend on honor, respect, and care invested in its making."

darshini.heading: The darshini format
darshini.subtitle: We didn't just bring the dosa. We brought the entire culture.

items.title: Standing tables only
items.description: No chairs. Stand, eat, leave — the darshini way.

items.title: Quirky nicknames
items.description: You're called "Sultanpalya Shakir" or "Koramangala Karthik" — not a number.

items.title: Quick self-service
items.description: Made to order. Served in minutes. No fuss.

items.title: 275 square feet
items.description: One tiny space. One legendary dosa.

items.title: 100% vegetarian
items.description: Pure veg, like every darshini in Bangalore.

milestones.text: Akhil trains on Bengaluru's Avenue Road under a dosa master.
Benne opens in Bandra West. 275 sq ft, standing tables, instant queues.
Deepika Padukone, Ranveer Singh, Anushka Sharma, Virat Kohli visit. Media coverage explodes.
Second location opens on Gulmohar Road, Juhu.

founders.heading: The founders

cards.name: Akhil Iyer
cards.bio: Bengaluru native who spent 11 years in Mumbai missing darshini dosas. Trained on Avenue Road to master the craft. Turned homesickness into a restaurant.

cards.name: Shriya Narayan
cards.bio: Believes food is comfort, connection, and home. Brought the heart, the soul, and the business sense to every decision at Benne.

founders.alsoVisited: Also visited by: Dia Mirza, Masaba Gupta, and thousands of dosa lovers across Mumbai.

cta.heading: Ready to taste it?
cta.subtitle: Browse the menu or come visit us in person.
